title: Continuous Murmur - Patent Ductus Arteriosus
chief_complaint: Extremely preterm female (28 weeks, 1000g) with continuous machinery murmur at left infraclavicular area, bounding pulses, and wide pulse pressure (BP 60/30).

physical_exam.heart: Continuous machinery murmur at left infraclavicular area, bounding pulses, hyperdynamic precordium

hidden_data.echo: Large PDA 3mm with continuous left-to-right shunt, LA:Ao ratio 1.8, dilated left atrium and left ventricle

key_learning_points: Surgical ligation if medical therapy fails

feedback.correct_message: Well diagnosed! Hemodynamically significant PDA in preterm.
feedback.incorrect_message: This is a PDA. The continuous murmur + echo confirm it.
feedback.attending_note: PDA management: Ibuprofen (10-5-5mg/kg) or indomethacin course. Fluid restriction. If medical therapy fails or contraindicated, surgical ligation. Most PDAs in preterm close spontaneously or with medical therapy.
